text:
Yasuha (ヤスハ, Yasuha) was a female servant of the Zoldyck Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 322 

Appearance

Yasuha has short light purple hair and is seen wearing the standard Zoldyck butler uniform. 

Personality

Though not much is known about Yasuha, it is briefly shown that she has a tendency to be very greedy, to the point of wishing to become a millionaire. When questioned about the incident, the angst-ridden Yasuha admits that she could not help herself. 

Background

She was a butler for the Zoldyck estate. 

Plot

13 th Hunter Chairman Election arc

Yasuha had the duty of looking after Alluka after the previous servant Mitsuba was crushed to death by Alluka's power. One day, Yasuha gave Alluka a piggyback ride at Alluka's request, and the child proceeded into wish-granting mode, recognizable by Alluka's pitch-black eyes and mouth. Succumbing to her covetous, Yasuha wished to become a millionaire to which Alluka agreed to. Suddenly, a rain of banknotes fell down onto them from a blimp in the sky. Later, it was reported that a currency transport vessel had suddenly gone missing, and when it was found, all the money had disappeared. 

What happened to her after this incident is unknown. Given the fact that the Zoldycks have extremely strict rules set out for their servants, Yasuha was presumably executed. 

The cost of her wish was afterward transferred to Kasuga and caused the deaths of her and 66 other people. 

Abilities & Powers

As a butler of the Zoldyck Family, Yasuha was in constant training. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 40 She could also open the Testing Gates, since according to Zebro, opening the gates is the bare minimum, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 39 although the number of doors she could open is unknown.

Nen

Yasuha also likely knew how to use Nen, since Canary, an apprentice butler, was a Nen user.

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Yasuha shares the same voice actress Megumi Han as Gon Freecss in the 2011 anime adaptation.

Intertextuality and References

In a page of Chapter 322 during the flashback involving Yasuha, several panels appear to reference the character Kirie in various chapters of Junji Ito's Uzumaki
The "flattened-to-death" deaths of the Zoldyck's butlers in Chapter 322's flashback mirror the deaths of various characters in Junji Ito's Smashed (alt. name Splatter Film).
